figures.caption: Fig. 4. Gaussian scale sweep. The privacy defect exhibits an interior minimizer, highlighting the two-parameter design nature of the Gaussian family.
Fig. 3. Laplace concentration sweep. At fixed sparsity, increasing λ improves utility but can worsen privacy after a point.
Fig. 1. Laplace support-size tradeoff for (ε, λ,H) = (1, 0.8, 2). The exact privacy defect decreases with s, while the first distortion moment increases.
Fig. 2. Gaussian support-size tradeoff for (ε, σ,H) = (1, 1.5, 2). The same privacy–distortion tension appears, but privacy decreases more slowly with s than in the Laplace case.
